initial_program:
% Determine the grid dimensions
RowMin(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R, _, _)}.
RowMax(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
ColMin(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_, C, _)}.
ColMax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Cells on the outer border (any cell on the outermost row or column)
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = Rmin.
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = Rmax.
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = Cmin.
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = Cmax.

% Fourconnected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that are reachable from the border through a path of zeros
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), boundary_cell(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                   R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, input(R2,C2,0).

% Every position must receive exactly one colour
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Nonzero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Actual), Actual != V.

% Reachable zeros stay 0
:- input(R,C,0), reachable(R,C), output(R,C,Actual), Actual != 0.

% Interior zeros become colour 3
:- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C), output(R,C,Actual), Actual != 3.

% Determine the grid dimensions
RowMin(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{R : input(R, _, _)}.
RowMax(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
ColMin(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{C : input(_, C, _)}.
ColMax(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Cells on the outer border (any cell on the outermost row or column)
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = Rmin.
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = Rmax.
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = Cmin.
boundary_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = Cmax.

% Fourconnected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1).  delta(0,-1).  delta(1,0).  delta(-1,0).

% Zeros that are reachable from the border through a path of zeros
reachable(R,C) :- input(R,C,0), boundary_cell(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                   R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC, input(R2,C2,0).

% Every position must receive exactly one colour
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Nonzero cells keep their original colour
:- input(R,C,V), V != 0, output(R,C,Actual), Actual != V.

% Reachable zeros stay 0
:- input(R,C,0), reachable(R,C), output(R,C,Actual), Actual != 0.

% Interior zeros become colour 3
:- input(R,C,0), not reachable(R,C), output(R,C,Actual), Actual != 3.
